title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy and savory Korean seafood pancake. This easy recipe guides you to perfect results at home. Get ready to cook!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A freshly made Haemul Pajeon, a traditional Korean seafood pancake, with a crispy golden crust and visible seafood pieces.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this delicious Haemul Pajeon, a perfect Korean seafood pancake for any meal!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake loaded with fresh seafood and crisp green onions, pan-fried to golden perfection. Its crispy edges and tender interior make it an irresistible snack or side dish, often enjoyed with a tangy dipping sauce. This classic dish offers a delightful blend of textures and flavors, embodying the vibrant spirit of Korean cuisine.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters; chopped into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and sugar. Gradually add the cold water and beaten egg, mixing until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are fine. Set aside in the refrigerator to chill for 10 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and pat dry the mixed seafood. Cut any larger pieces of shrimp or squid into bite-sized portions. Cut the green onions into 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili (if using).

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, green onions, and sliced yellow onion to the chilled batter. Gently fold everything together until the seafood and vegetables are evenly coated.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Ensure the pan is hot before adding the batter to achieve a crispy texture.

instructions.title: Cook the First Side
instructions.text: Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake about 1/2 inch thick. Arrange some of the green onion pieces nicely on top if desired. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake. Using a large spatula, flip the pancake. Cook for another 4-5 minutes on the second side, pressing down gently, until it is golden brown and cooked through. Repeat for the remaining batter.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: While the pancakes cook, combine all dipping sauce ingredients (soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, sesame oil, minced garlic, toasted sesame seeds, chili flakes) in a small bowl and mix well.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice into wedges. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce on the side for a delightful Korean culinary experience.

tips: Use very cold water or even sparkling water for the batter to achieve an extra crispy pancake.
Do not overmix the batter; lumps are perfectly fine and help create a lighter texture.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispiness. It pairs wonderfully with Makgeolli (Korean rice wine).
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to Korea with one of its most beloved and comforting dishes: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake. This savory, crispy, and incredibly flavorful pancake is a staple in Korean households, a popular street food, and a go-to anju (drinking snack) in bustling pubs. If you've ever craved a dish that perfectly balances a satisfying crunch with tender, succulent seafood and the fresh bite of green onions, then Haemul Pajeon is an absolute must-try. Easy to make at home and bursting with authentic Korean flavors, it’s a dish that promises to delight your taste buds and transport you straight to the vibrant streets of Seoul.

## What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?
At its heart, Haemul Pajeon is a type of jeon (Korean pancake) distinguished by its generous inclusion of "haemul" (seafood) and "pa" (green onions). Unlike thinner, crêpe-like pancakes, pajeon boasts a substantial, chewy interior encased in a wonderfully crispy, golden-brown crust. The star of the show, beyond the vibrant green onions, is the medley of fresh seafood—typically shrimp, squid, and sometimes mussels or oysters—which adds a delightful brininess and tender texture to every bite.

Originating as a simple dish made from available ingredients, Haemul Pajeon has evolved into a sophisticated yet approachable comfort food. It's often enjoyed as a hearty appetizer, a light meal, or a perfect accompaniment to Makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or Soju. The beauty of this dish lies in its contrast: the sweetness of the green onions, the umami from the seafood, and the slight chewiness of the batter all come together in a harmonious symphony of flavors and textures, made even better with a tangy, spicy dipping sauce.

## Ingredients You Need
Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon requires a few key components, but don't be intimidated—they are all readily available.

**For the Batter:**
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/4 cup rice flour (for extra crispiness, optional but recommended)
*   1 1/2 cups very cold water or anchovy broth (cold liquid is key for a crispy batter)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

**For the Seafood:**
*   4 oz shrimp, peeled, deveined, and chopped into bite-sized pieces
*   4 oz squid, cleaned and sliced into rings or bite-sized pieces
*   (Optional) 2 oz mussels, shucked, or small pieces of imitation crab meat

**For the Vegetables:**
*   1 large bunch (about 8-10 stalks) green onions (scallions), cut into 2-3 inch lengths, some halved lengthwise
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced
*   1/4 cup thinly sliced carrot or red bell pepper (for color and extra flavor, optional)

**For Frying:**
*   Vegetable oil or any neutral oil, generous amount

**For the Dipping Sauce:**
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), adjust to taste
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon roasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced
*   (Optional) 1 teaspoon finely chopped green onion

## Step-by-Step Instructions
Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Haemul Pajeon in your own kitchen.

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, whisk together all the dipping sauce ingredients. Set aside.
2.  **Prepare Seafood and Vegetables:** Pat the shrimp and squid very dry with paper towels to prevent the pancake from becoming soggy. If using frozen seafood, ensure it's fully thawed and drained. Prepare your green onions and other vegetables as directed in the ingredients list.
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (if using), salt, and pepper. Gradually add the very cold water or broth while whisking until just combined. Add the egg and whisk lightly. It's okay if there are a few lumps; overmixing can lead to a tough pancake.
4.  **Combine Ingredients:** Gently fold the prepared seafood, sliced onion, and most of the cut green onions into the batter. Reserve a handful of the longer green onion pieces for arranging on top of the pancake during cooking.
5.  **Cook the Pancake:** Heat a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 3-4 tablespoons of oil, ensuring the bottom of the pan is well coated. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, pour about half of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake.
6.  **Arrange Green Onions:** Immediately arrange the reserved longer green onion pieces on top of the batter in a parallel fashion, pressing them lightly into the batter. This not only looks beautiful but ensures the green onions are well-distributed and cooked.
7.  **Fry to Golden Perfection:** Let the pancake cook undisturbed for about 4-5 minutes, or until the edges are crispy and golden brown and the batter looks mostly set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake.
8.  **Flip and Finish:** Carefully slide a wide spatula under the pancake and flip it over. Cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side, pressing down gently with the spatula to ensure even contact with the pan and to enhance crispiness. The Haemul Pajeon should be deeply golden brown and crispy on both sides, and the seafood cooked through.
9.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked pancake to a cutting board. Cut it into wedges or squares using a pizza cutter or knife. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan as needed. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
Achieving that perfect crispy-chewy balance in your Haemul Pajeon is key. Here are a few expert tips:

*   **Cold is Gold:** Using very cold water or even sparkling water for the batter helps to create a lighter, crispier texture by inhibiting gluten development.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil:** Pajeon needs a generous amount of oil to develop its signature crispy crust. Think shallow frying, not just light greasing.
*   **High Heat Start:** Begin with medium-high heat to get a good sear on the first side, then you can slightly reduce it if the pancake is browning too quickly before the inside cooks.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat it thoroughly dry with paper towels.
*   **Rice Flour Boost:** Adding rice flour to your batter is a secret weapon for an extra crispy exterior that stays crisp longer.
*   **Don't Overmix:** A slightly lumpy batter is better than an overmixed one. Overmixing develops gluten, leading to a tough pancake.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed piping hot and fresh off the pan when it’s at its crispiest.

## Serving Suggestions
Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways:

*   **As an Appetizer:** It makes a fantastic sharing appetizer for any meal, especially with friends and family.
*   **With Makgeolli or Soju:** This is the classic pairing! The savory, slightly greasy nature of the pancake perfectly complements the sweet and refreshing qualities of Korean rice wine or the clean taste of Soju.
*   **Light Meal:** Paired with a bowl of rice and some simple banchan (Korean side dishes like kimchi or pickled radishes), it can make a satisfying light lunch or dinner.
*   **Party Food:** Cut into smaller squares, it's an excellent finger food for gatherings and parties.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a delight, don't hesitate to experiment:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Omit the seafood entirely and load up on an assortment of vegetables like sliced mushrooms (shiitake or oyster), zucchini, bell peppers, or even kimchi for a delicious kimchi pajeon.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** Add about 1/2 cup of chopped, well-squeezed kimchi to the batter for a tangy, spicy kick.
*   **Meat Pajeon:** Thinly sliced pork belly or beef bulgogi meat can be added for a heartier, meaty pancake.
*   **Different Seafood:** Feel free to customize your seafood mix based on preference and availability. Clams, oysters, or even tiny scallops would work wonderfully.
*   **Spicier Batter:** For those who love heat, add a teaspoon of gochujang (Korean chili paste) or extra gochugaru to the batter itself.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** Use a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend along with rice flour for a gluten-free version.

Whether you're new to Korean cuisine or a seasoned fan, making Haemul Pajeon at home is a rewarding experience. This iconic seafood pancake offers a delightful combination of textures and flavors that is both comforting and exciting. So gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to enjoy a taste of Korea that will undoubtedly become a new favorite!
